msgs.text:
Turn 1 — Lean formalization plan for the GQ2 presentation I read the protocol and problem files, the official FrontierMath statement, and the manuscript/PDF/TEX content. The lane is explicitly to produce an inline Lean 4/mathlib implementation plan, not to carry out the formalization in this turn. The target is a machine-checked proof that the manuscript’s four-generator profinite presentation is correct, using the coding agent and the 100-core remote Lean compile backend.The official problem asks for a profinite presentation of Gal(Q2/Q2), with distinguished tame generators and later generators required to lie in the 2-core; it also specifies the submission-style word serialization convention for finite verification.

The manuscript’s actual theorem states that GQ2 is generated by σ,τ,x0,x1,with the closed normal subgroup generated by x0,x1 pro-2, tame relation τσ=τ2, and wild relationh0u1−1x1σc0=1 using the auxiliary words σ2,ui,d0,z0,c0,g0,dg,hc,h0.The proof strategy is not a syntactic presentation argument alone: it constructs the candidate from admissible finite quotients, proves equality of finite epimorphism counts, and then reconstructs the profinite group.

1. Formalization stance

The central design choice should be:Do not begin by building a general free profinite group presentation API.Instead, formalize exactly the manuscript’s finite-quotient semantics. Define ΓA as the inverse limit of finite marked quotients satisfying the two relators and the 2-core condition, then prove that its epimorphisms to finite groups are exactly the admissible generating quadruples. This matches Proposition 2.3 and avoids a very large detour through general profinite presentations.The final Lean theorem should be an isomorphism in ProfiniteGrp, plus a user-facing ContinuousMulEquiv version.

Schematic Lean target:lean import Mathlib.FieldTheory.Galois.Profinite import Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Category.ProfiniteGrp.Basic import Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Category.ProfiniteGrp.Limits import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.PadicNumbers noncomputable section open scoped Classical namespace Q2Presentation abbrev Q2 : Type := ℚ_[2]abbrev Q2bar : Type := AlgebraicClosure Q2 abbrev GQ2 : Type := Q2bar ≃ₐ[Q2] Q2bar/-- The candidate profinite group defined as the inverse limit of admissible finite marked quotients satisfying the Q₂ presentation. -/noncomputable def GammaA : ProfiniteGrp := by -- inverse limit of admissible finite quotients exact Candidate.limit/-- Absolute Galois group of Q₂ as a profinite group. -/noncomputable def GQ2Profinite : ProfiniteGrp :=InfiniteGalois.profiniteGalGrp Q2 Q2bar/-- Main theorem: the candidate presentation is correct. -/theorem presentation_correct :Nonempty (GammaA ≅ GQ2Profinite) := by -- finite epimorphism counts + profinite reconstruction exact main_iso/-- Equivalent topological group statement. -/theorem presentation_correct_continuousMulEquiv :Nonempty ((GammaA : Type) ≃ₜ* GQ2) := by -- unwrap `presentation_correct` exact main_continuousMulEquiv end Q2Presentation Mathlib already has InfiniteGalois.ProfiniteGalGrp for the Galois group as a profinite group and proves that the Galois group is continuously isomorphic to the inverse limit of finite Galois groups.Lean Community Mathlib also has ProfiniteGrp, finite groups as profinite groups, closed subgroups, and limits in ProfiniteGrp, which are exactly the ambient structures needed for the candidate inverse-limit construction.Lean Community

2. Core definitions to build first 2.1 Generators, words, and finite ω2 -power evaluation

Define the generator type:lean inductive Gen | sigma | tau | x0 | x1 deriving DecidableEq, Fintype

Use the manuscript’s right-conjugation convention:xg=g−1 xg,[x,y]=x−1y−1xy.lean def rconj [Group G] (x g : G) : G := g⁻¹ * x * g def comm [Group G] (x y : G) : G := x⁻¹ * y⁻¹ * x * y

For finite groups, define the ω2 -power by reducing the profinite idempotent modulo the finite exponent. The manuscript’s Appendix C gives a finite verification modulus and a serialized residue, but the proof itself uses the genuine profinite exponent; the formalization should mirror this by making the finite-target power depend only on the exponent of the finite target, not on the fixed verification modulus.

Implementation object:lean/-- The idempotent selecting the 2-primary component, evaluated modulo `n`. -/def omega2Mod (n : ℕ) : ZMod n := ...

Required lemmas:lean theorem omega2Mod_eq_one_on_two_part ...theorem omega2Mod_eq_zero_on_odd_part ...theorem finite_omega2_pow_idempotent [Finite G] [Group G] (g : G) :powOmega2 (powOmega2 g) = powOmega2 g := ...theorem finite_omega2_pow_independent_of_rep ...Then define the candidate words as functions of a marked tuple q:Gen→G:lean def sigma2 (q : Gen → G) : G := powOmega2 (q Gen.sigma)def u0 (q : Gen → G) : G := powOmega2 (q Gen.x0 * q Gen.tau)def u1 (q : Gen → G) : G := powOmega2 (q Gen.x1 * q Gen.tau)def d0 (q : Gen → G) : G := u0 q * (q Gen.x0)⁻¹ def z0 (q : Gen → G) : G := rconj (q Gen.x0) (sigma2 q)def c0 (q : Gen → G) : G := comm (d0 q) (z0 q)def g0 (q : Gen → G) : G := (sigma2 q)^2 def dg (q : Gen → G) : G := rconj (d0 q) (g0 q)def hc (q : Gen → G) : G := comm (dg q) (d0 q)def h0 (q : Gen → G) : G :=rconj (q Gen.x0) (g0 q) * q Gen.x0 * dg q * d0 q * (d0 q)^2 * hc q def tameRel (q : Gen → G) : Prop :=rconj (q Gen.tau) (q Gen.sigma) = (q Gen.tau)^2 def wildRel (q : Gen → G) : Prop :=h0 q * (u1 q)⁻¹ * rconj (q Gen.x1) (q Gen.sigma) * c0 q = 1 2.2 The 2-core and admissible quotients

Implement O2 (G) for finite groups as the largest normal 2-subgroup.lean def pCore (p : ℕ) (G : Type*) [Group G] : Subgroup G := ...abbrev twoCore (G : Type*) [Group G] : Subgroup G := pCore 2 G

Needed API:lean theorem twoCore_normal : (twoCore G).Normal := ...theorem mem_twoCore_iff_normal_closure_is_two_group ...theorem hom_image_twoCore_le_twoCore ...theorem quotient_by_twoCore_has_no_nontrivial_normal_two_subgroup ...

Then:lean structure AdmissibleMarkedQuotient where G : Type groupG : Group G finiteG : Fintype G q : Gen → G generates : Subgroup.closure (Set.range q) = ⊤ tame : tameRel q wild : wildRel q x0_mem_twoCore : q Gen.x0 ∈ twoCore G x1_mem_twoCore : q Gen.x1 ∈ twoCore G This is the formal kernel of the presentation. Once this is correct, all later maps to finite targets reduce to finite algebra.

3. Staged formalization plan Stage A — Repository skeleton and finite algebra foundations

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Foundation/Omega2.lean Q2Presentation/Foundation/RightConj.lean Q2Presentation/Foundation/TwoCore.lean Q2Presentation/Foundation/FiniteSurj.lean Q2Presentation/Foundation/MarkedWords.lean

Main deliverables:lean theorem omega2_pow_finite_quotient_compatible ...theorem tameRel_quotient ...theorem wildRel_quotient ...theorem twoCore_subdirect_product ...theorem generating_tuple_subdirect ...Infra use: fan out #check and small proof searches across the cluster for every likely mathlib theorem about Subgroup.normalClosure, finite quotients, Sylow, normal subgroups, and group exponent. Give the GPT-5.5-Pro coding agent only narrow jobs: “prove this lemma with these imports; do not change the statement.” Compile 20–40 proof attempts in parallel, keep the shortest passing one, then rerun simp?, aesop?, exact?, and linarith variants on the successful branch.

Stage B — Construct ΓA and prove finite-quotient semantics

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Candidate/Admissible.lean Q2Presentation/Candidate/InverseLimit.lean Q2Presentation/Candidate/EpiSemantics.lean Q2Presentation/Candidate/Reconstruction.lean

Manuscript mapping:Lemma 2.1: finite subdirect closure.Lemma 2.2: cofinality; WA is pro-2.Proposition 2.3: epimorphisms ΓA ↠G are generating quadruples satisfying the two relations and x0,x1∈O2(G).Lemma 2.5: one-sided profinite reconstruction from equality of finite surjection counts.

Candidate construction should be:lean noncomputable def admissibleDiagram : CategoryTheory.Functor AdmissibleIndex ProfiniteGrp := ...noncomputable def GammaA : ProfiniteGrp := limit admissibleDiagram

Main theorems:lean theorem GammaA_epi_iff_admissible (G : Type*) [Group G] [Fintype G] :SurjContHom GammaA (ProfiniteGrp.ofFiniteGrp (FiniteGrp.of G))≃ { q : Gen → G // Subgroup.closure (Set.range q) = ⊤∧ tameRel q ∧ wildRel q∧ q Gen.x0 ∈ twoCore G∧ q Gen.x1 ∈ twoCore G } := ...theorem profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts (P Q : ProfiniteGrp)(hfg : TopologicallyFinitelyGenerated P)(h : ∀ (G : Type*) [Group G] [Fintype G],Fintype.card (SurjContHom P (ofFinite G))= Fintype.card (SurjContHom Q (ofFinite G))) :Nonempty (P ≅ Q) := ...Mathlib’s ProfiniteGrp limit and finite quotient API should be reused here, especially the theorem that a profinite group is the projective limit of its finite quotients.Lean Community Infra use: this stage is ideal for massive compile fan-out because most lemmas are independent finite-group facts. The coding agent should be given the exact manuscript statement plus an already-defined Lean signature; the cluster should compile variants using different proof strategies. Run a continuous “no hidden axioms” job on this stage:lean

#print axioms GammaA_epi_iff_admissible

#print axioms profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts Stage C — Tame quotient, maximal pro-2 quotient, and full unramified marking

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Boundary/Tame.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/DemushkinMarked.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/Pro2Quotient.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/FiberProduct.lean

Manuscript mapping:Lemma 3.1 and Proposition 3.2: tame quotient.Lemma 3.3: wild subgroup is the characteristic 2-core.Proposition 1.1: marked dyadic Demushkin normalization.Proposition 3.10: maximal pro-2 quotient Π=⟨σ,x0,x1∣x0σ2x0[x1,σ]=1⟩pro-2.Corollary 3.12 and Proposition 3.14: full unramified marking, not merely mod 2.

Critical Lean theorems:lean def Ttame : ProfiniteGrp := ...def Pi : ProfiniteGrp := ...def nu_t : Ttame ⟶ ProfiniteZ2 := ...def nu_2 : Pi ⟶ ProfiniteZ2 := ...theorem GammaA_tame_quotient :QuotientProfinite GammaA GammaA.Wild ≅ Ttame := ...theorem GQ2_tame_quotient :QuotientProfinite GQ2Profinite WildInertia ≅ Ttame := ...theorem GammaA_maximal_pro2 :MaximalPro2Quotient GammaA ≅ Pi := ...theorem local_marked_demushkin :MaximalPro2Quotient GQ2Profinite ≅ Pi -- preserving νur : _ ⟶ ℤ₂:= ...theorem tame_pro2_markings_compatible :-- ν_t and ν_2 agree with the same unramified character...This is the first genuinely high-risk stage. Mathlib has p-adic numbers, local field structures, Galois groups as profinite groups, cyclotomic characters, and ramification/inertia infrastructure.Lean Community+3 Lean Community+3 Lean Community+3 But the exact ingredients used in the manuscript—Labute’s Demushkin classification, the Q2 -specific marked local reciprocity calculation, and the full integral unramified marking—are not likely to exist in mathlib as ready-made theorems.

Implementation tactic: formalize only the specialized Q2 theorem needed by the manuscript, not a general Demushkin theory. A good interface is:lean theorem Q2_marked_Demushkin :∃ (a s y : MaximalPro2Quotient GQ2Profinite),TopologicalClosureSubgroup.closure {a,s,y} = ⊤∧ a^2 * s^4 * comm s y = 1∧ nu_ur a = -2∧ nu_ur s = 1∧ nu_ur y = 0 := ...Then prove the Nielsen transform to Π purely algebraically. The manuscript emphasizes that the full Z2 -marking is load-bearing for the boundary and terminal pro-2 comparison.Infra use: split this into three independent tracks: finite tame quotient lemmas, pro-2 Nielsen algebra, and local Q2 theorem. The first two should be cluster-fanned. The third should be driven by the coding agent in theorem-interface layers: first state all exact local facts, then formalize the short algebraic consequences, then fill the local facts one by one.

Stage D — Boundary-framed exact-image theorem

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Boundary/BoundaryFramed.lean Q2Presentation/Boundary/ExactImage.lean

Manuscript mapping:

Define∂bd=Ttame×Z2Π.

Define boundary-framed targets (Y,LY,πY,θY).Theorem 4.2: equality of boundary-framed exact-image counts.

Lean objects:lean def Boundary : ProfiniteGrp := pullback nu_t nu_2 structure BoundaryFramedTarget where Y : Type groupY : Group Y finiteY : Fintype Y LY : Subgroup Y LY_normal : LY.Normal LY_isTwoGroup : IsPGroup 2 LY H : Type groupH : Group H finiteH : Fintype H piY : Y →* H piY_surj : Function.Surjective piY ker_piY : piY.ker = LY E : Type addCommGroupE : AddCommGroup E vectorE : Module (ZMod 2) E finiteE : Fintype E thetaY : Y →* E

Main theorem:lean theorem boundary_framed_exact_image (Y : BoundaryFramedTarget) :exactCount GammaA Y = exactCount GQ2Profinite Y := ...This theorem will remain a high-level target until Stages E–H are complete.Infra use: generate all “exact-image partition” lemmas and subgroup inheritance lemmas as standalone finite group tasks. These are excellent for compile farm fan-out because they are independent of Q2.

Stage E — Candidate-side linear lifting theory

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Lifting/SemidirectWords.lean Q2Presentation/Lifting/FoxJacobian.lean Q2Presentation/Lifting/Heisenberg.lean Q2Presentation/Lifting/Stokes.lean Q2Presentation/Lifting/CandidateDuality.lean

Manuscript mapping:Lemma 5.3: h0 may replace x02 in linear, cup-product, and determinant calculations.Lemma 5.4 and 5.5: finite-word ledger and evaluated Jacobian.Proposition 5.8: traced Stokes identity; the trace over the two relators is essential.Proposition 5.10: Fox–Heisenberg chain map.Lemmas 5.11–5.13 and Proposition 5.15: exact cone dévissage and candidate deformation duality.

Lean design:lean def WordComplex (ρ : LowerMap C) (A : Rep (ZMod 2) C) :CochainComplex (ModuleCat (ZMod 2)) ℕ := ...def d0_word : A →ₗ[ZMod 2] A^4 := ...def d1_word : A^4 →ₗ[ZMod 2] A^2 := ...theorem evaluated_jacobian_simple_tame :d1_word = ![tameRow, wildRow] := ...def heisenbergPairing : WordComplex ρ A ⟶ dualShift (WordComplex ρ Aᵛ) := ...theorem candidate_duality :IsQuasiIso (heisenbergPairing ρ A) := ...This stage should avoid mathlib’s general group cohomology where the candidate side is concerned: the manuscript uses an explicit three-term complex from the two relators.Formalize that directly.Infra use: this is the best place for executable regression tests. Define finite groups and modules, evaluate the words, and compare to the stated linear maps by native_decide for small cases. Then prove the general symbolic lemmas. Let the coding agent produce one lemma per word factor and one lemma per ledger row; compile them in parallel.

Stage F — Local cohomology interface and local Tate duality

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Local/ContinuousCohomologyLowDegree.lean Q2Presentation/Local/TateDualityQ2.lean Q2Presentation/Local/HilbertSymbolQ2.lean Q2Presentation/Local/SquareClassFiltration.lean

Manuscript mapping:Proposition 5.16: local lifting duality.Corollary 5.17: common lift numerics and adjoint connecting maps.Lemma 6.17 and Proposition 6.18: local determinant calculation and Gauss sign comparison.Mathlib has both ordinary group cohomology and continuous cohomology infrastructure. Ordinary group cohomology includes inhomogeneous cochains, low-degree API, and the definition of Hn (G,A).Lean Community Continuous cohomology exists, but the docs currently mark important missing API such as usual n-ary cochains, short exact sequence long exact sequences, and comparison with group cohomology for discrete groups.Lean Community Implementation tactic: build a local low-degree continuous cohomology wrapper specialized to profinite groups acting on finite discrete F2 -modules. Use continuous maps into finite discrete modules, then prove that every continuous cochain factors through a finite quotient. This makes the local side compatible with the finite target induction.

Core interface:lean def Hcont0 (Γ : ProfiniteGrp) (A : FiniteDiscreteRep Γ) := ...def Hcont1 (Γ : ProfiniteGrp) (A : FiniteDiscreteRep Γ) := ...def Hcont2 (Γ : ProfiniteGrp) (A : FiniteDiscreteRep Γ) := ...theorem Q2_local_Tate_duality (A : FiniteDiscreteRep GQ2Profinite) :PerfectPairing (Hcont i GQ2Profinite A)(Hcont (2-i) GQ2Profinite A.dual)(ZMod 2) := ...theorem Q2_local_EulerPoincare (A : FiniteDiscreteRep GQ2Profinite) :dim_Z1 = 2 * dim A + dim A.dual.invariants := ...This is another high-risk stage. Keep it Q2 -specific. Avoid proving global local Tate duality in full generality.Infra use: proof development here is not broad finite search; it is theorem-proving with long dependency chains. Use the cluster to run alternate import minimizations and to verify that local theorem interfaces do not pull the whole repository into every downstream finite-group file.

Stage G — Quadratic determinant obstructions

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Quadratic/ExtraspecialCocycles.lean Q2Presentation/Quadratic/ShapiroCorestriction.lean Q2Presentation/Quadratic/EvensNorm.lean Q2Presentation/Quadratic/CandidateGauss.lean Q2Presentation/Quadratic/LocalGauss.lean Q2Presentation/Quadratic/AffineTerms.lean

Manuscript mapping:Lemma 6.1: normalized equivariant extraspecial cocycle.Lemma 6.13: universal two-point D8 class and Evens norm.Lemma 6.15: quadratic orbit–stabilizer Shapiro.Proposition 6.5 and 6.9: candidate base determinant form and zero count.Lemma 6.16–6.18: local deep-unit calculation and dyadic base determinant theorem.Lemma 6.21–6.22: determinant transgression and marking-preserving shear.

The key formal object:lean structure QuadraticSpaceF2 where V : Type addCommGroup : AddCommGroup V module : Module (ZMod 2) V finite : Fintype V q : V → ZMod 2 polar : V →ₗ[ZMod 2] V →ₗ[ZMod 2] ZMod 2 polar_def : ...nonsingular : ...def arfInvariant (Q : QuadraticSpaceF2) : ZMod 2 := ...def gaussSumSign (Q : QuadraticSpaceF2) : Z := ...

Main comparison theorem:lean theorem determinant_base_gauss_same (V : SimpleSelfDualTameModule C)(q : InvariantNonsingularQuadraticForm V) :gaussSum candidateBaseForm = gaussSum localBaseForm := ...The manuscript’s determinant section is where convention mistakes are most dangerous: normalized cochains, left/right actions, Shapiro maps, and corestriction are all fixed at cochain level in Appendix A, precisely to prevent silent sign or section errors.In Lean, make those conventions definitional, not notational.Infra use: turn every formula in Appendix A into a theorem with executable left-hand/right-hand finite cochain evaluation. Use the coding agent to produce local calculations, but require the cluster to run randomized finite tests before attempting symbolic proof. For example, for κJ, test d κJ = 0 for all elements of E2⋊C2 by exhaustive decidable computation, then prove it abstractly.Stage H — Finite target induction, Fourier inversion, exact-image recursion

Lean files:Q2Presentation/Induction/BoundaryTargets.lean Q2Presentation/Induction/ScalarCovers.lean Q2Presentation/Induction/Fourier.lean Q2Presentation/Induction/ExactImageRecursion.lean Q2Presentation/Induction/TerminalCase.lean Q2Presentation/Induction/Main.lean

Manuscript mapping:Section 7: isolate a minimal non-scalar module layer.Section 8: central covers, affine fibres, Fourier inversion, exact-image recursion.Theorem 8.17: closed exact-image recursion.Section 9: proof of boundary-framed theorem.Section 10: sum over tame frames to get all finite epimorphism counts.

Core Lean theorem:lean theorem closed_exact_image_recursion (Y : BoundaryFramedTarget)(h_nonScalar : HasNonScalarChiefFactor Y.LY) :exactCount Γ Y =recursionExpression Γ Y := ...theorem boundary_framed_exact_image_main (Y : BoundaryFramedTarget) :exactCount GammaA Y = exactCount GQ2Profinite Y := by induction h : Fintype.card Y.LY using Nat.strong_induction_on with | _ n ih => by_cases hterminal : OnlyTrivialChiefFactors Y.LY· exact terminal_case hterminal· exact nonterminal_case ih hterminal

Then:lean theorem finite_surjection_counts_equal (G : Type*) [Group G] [Fintype G] :Fintype.card (SurjContHom GammaA (ofFinite G))=Fintype.card (SurjContHom GQ2Profinite (ofFinite G)) := ...theorem presentation_correct :Nonempty (GammaA ≅ GQ2Profinite) :=profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts GammaA GQ2Profinite GammaA_topologically_finitely_generated finite_surjection_counts_equal The manuscript’s terminal case depends on the fully marked pro-2 quotient; the plan must not weaken the marking to mod 2.Infra use: this stage is mostly finite group, finite module, and cardinal arithmetic. It should be heavily parallelized. Ask the coding agent for lemma decomposition of each boxed recursion identity, then farm every subgroup/cardinality lemma independently.

4. How to exploit the 100-core remote Lean backend Use the cluster as a proof-search and compile oracle, not just a faster CI machine.4.1 Build pipeline

Maintain three branches:main-clean: no sorry, no local axioms except Lean/mathlib standard axioms.skeleton: theorem statements with by sorry, used to validate dependency order.proof-lab: thousands of short-lived proof-attempt branches.

Each job contains:JSON{ "file": "Q2Presentation/Lifting/FoxJacobian.lean","theorem": "wild_row_eq","imports_hash": "...","goal_state": "...","attempt_id": 17}

Worker command:Bash lake env lean Q2Presentation/Lifting/FoxJacobian.lean

Collect:success/failure,full Lean error,proof-state around failure,heartbeats/time,imports used.4.2 GPT-5.5-Pro coding-agent usage

Give the agent constrained tasks:“Here is the theorem statement and error. Produce only a replacement proof term.” “Split this theorem into 5 lemmas with Lean signatures; do not prove them.” “Find likely mathlib names for this concept; produce #check probes.” “Replace this brittle proof with a more stable proof using only existing lemmas.” “Minimize imports for this file.” Do not ask it to invent broad mathematical arguments. The manuscript supplies the argument; the agent’s job is Lean translation and proof repair.4.3 Parallel fan-out by stage Stages A–B: 80–100 concurrent jobs. These are mostly finite-group API lemmas and quotient semantics.Stage C: split into a 60-core algebra lane and a 40-core local-theory lane. Algebraic Nielsen and tame quotient proofs compile quickly; local marked Demushkin work will need longer jobs.Stage E: use exhaustive finite tests before proofs. Generate random and complete checks for tiny finite groups/modules. Compile all generated theorem attempts independently.Stage F: lower fan-out, higher context. Use remote workers for import minimization, theorem-probe scripts, and long builds.Stage G: parallelize by formula. Every cochain identity, Shapiro identity, section-change formula, and finite quadratic identity should be its own file or subsection until stable.Stage H: heavy fan-out again. Most subgroup, exact-image, Fourier, and cardinality identities are independent.4.4 “No local waiting” pipeline While cluster jobs for Stage n run, the coding agent drafts Stage n+1 signatures against already stable interfaces. Failed jobs are automatically converted into prompts containing:lean -- imports -- theorem statement -- local context -- exact error -- allowed lemmas The only synchronous gate should be moving a theorem from proof-lab to main-clean.4.5 Trust checks

For every milestone theorem:lean

#print axioms theorem_name

Final acceptance requires:no sorry;no custom axiom or constant for mathematical facts;no hidden executable “verification” replacing a proof;

#print axioms Q2Presentation.presentation_correct showing only standard Lean/classical axioms.The manuscript explicitly says the separate computational verification of the displayed word is independent and not used in the mathematical proof, so in Lean it should be a regression test suite, not a proof dependency.

5. Recommended mathlib libraries and exact gaps Profinite and finite groupsUse:lean Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Category.ProfiniteGrp.Basic Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Category.ProfiniteGrp.Limits Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Category.ProfiniteGrp.Completion Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.FiniteGrp Mathlib.GroupTheory.QuotientGroup.Basic Mathlib.GroupTheory.Sylow Mathlib.GroupTheory.SchurZassenhaus Mathlib.GroupTheory.SemidirectProduct Mathlib’s ProfiniteGrp is the category of compact totally disconnected topological groups; it includes finite groups as profinite groups and has closed subgroup and limit infrastructure.Lean Community It also has profinite completion as a limit of finite quotients with a universal property.Lean Community

Gaps to fill:p-core Op (G) API.Pro-p normal closure semantics for finite quotients.Exact-image subgroup bookkeeping.Profinite Goursat and reconstruction lemma in the exact form needed.

Galois and p-adic fieldsUse:lean Mathlib.FieldTheory.Galois.Profinite Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.PadicNumbers Mathlib.NumberTheory.LocalField.Basic Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Galois Mathlib.NumberTheory.Cyclotomic.CyclotomicCharacter Mathlib defines p-adic numbers ℚ_[p] as the completion of Q, with valuation and completeness API.Lean Community It has a nonarchimedean local field class giving local compactness, discreteness, finite residue field, completeness, and value group facts.Lean Community It also has ramification/inertia theory for finite Galois extensions of Dedekind domains.Lean Community

Gaps to fill:local reciprocity for Q2;explicit square-class filtration of Q2 and tame extensions;dyadic Hilbert symbol formulas;local Tate duality and Euler–Poincaré for finite 2-torsion modules;

Labute/Demushkin classification and the marked Q2 normalization.The cyclotomic character file exists, but its docs indicate that compatibility of modular cyclotomic characters and full p-adic construction details still have TODO items, so this should be treated as reusable but incomplete for the marked Demushkin part.Lean Community

Presentations and wordsUse:lean Mathlib.GroupTheory.FreeGroup.Basic Mathlib.GroupTheory.PresentedGroup PresentedGroup constructs ordinary discrete groups as quotients of free groups by normal closures of relations.Lean Community

Gap to fill:profinite presentations are not the same as discrete presentations. Use finite-quotient semantics for ΓA, and only use FreeGroup/PresentedGroup for auxiliary word syntax, not as the main profinite object.

Cohomology and linear algebraUse:lean Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Homological.GroupCohomology.Basic Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Homological.GroupCohomology.LowDegree Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ContinuousCohomology.Basic Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FiniteDimensional Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.BilinearMap Mathlib.FieldTheory.Finite.Basic Ordinary group cohomology has inhomogeneous cochains and low-degree API.Lean Community Continuous cohomology exists, but its docs list missing comparisons and exact-sequence API, so the formalization should build a focused low-degree finite-discrete continuous cohomology layer.Lean Community

Gaps to fill:continuous cohomology long exact sequences for finite discrete modules;

Shapiro/corestriction/Evens norm at normalized cochain level;local Tate duality;extraspecial and Heisenberg central-extension APIs;quadratic forms over F2, Arf invariant, and Gauss sum counts.

6. Dependency order for final assembly

The clean dependency chain should be:

Omega2 + finite words + twoCore↓ Admissible finite quotients → GammaA → finite quotient semantics↓ Tame quotient + maximal pro-2 quotient + marked Q2 Demushkin theorem↓ Boundary fiber product ∂bd and boundary-framed targets↓ Candidate word complex + Fox–Heisenberg chain map↓ Local Tate duality and shared lifting numerics↓ Quadratic determinant obstruction and shared Gauss signs↓ Exact-image recursion and finite target induction↓ Boundary-framed exact-image equality↓ All finite surjection counts equal↓ Profinite reconstruction↓ GammaA ≅ GQ2Profinite This mirrors the manuscript’s own dependency certificate: finite-word Stokes identities feed the Fox–Heisenberg chain map; cone dévissage feeds candidate duality; normalized Shapiro/corestriction feeds the local determinant calculation; deep-unit Hilbert-symbol work feeds local hyperbolicity; determinant transgression and radical-edge variation feed the exact-image recursion; and the closed recursion feeds the boundary-framed theorem.

7. First concrete work packets

The first 10 work packets should be:Define Gen, rconj, comm, and the word evaluator.Define finite omega2Mod and prove finite exponent independence.Define twoCore and prove basic normality/functoriality.Define AdmissibleMarkedQuotient.Prove finite subdirect closure.Define GammaA as an inverse limit.Prove GammaA_epi_iff_admissible.Formalize profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts.Define Ttame and prove finite tame quotient structure.Define Pi and prove the candidate maximal pro-2 quotient reduces to the Nielsen-transformed Demushkin relator.Packets 1–8 are the best immediate cluster target: they exercise the infrastructure, expose missing mathlib API early, and produce the final theorem’s outer shell before entering the deep local theory.

8. Main risks and mitigation The biggest formalization gap is not the displayed word; it is the local Q2 input: marked Demushkin normalization, local Tate duality, Hilbert symbols, dyadic square-class filtration, and Evens-norm normalization. The mitigation is to formalize specialized Q2 theorem interfaces first, prove all finite-group and candidate-side consequences against those interfaces, and then discharge the local interfaces one by one.The second biggest risk is convention drift. The manuscript is unusually careful about right conjugation, left module action, normalized cochains, Shapiro maps, and transversals. In Lean, every convention should become a definition, and every section-change statement should be a theorem, not prose.The third risk is proof-search scale. The remote backend should be treated as a many-attempt proof compiler: many small files, small imports, automatic failure extraction, and no broad monolithic files until the end.
